name: KeyBot

consumes.minimum: 1 active comp + 1 selected layer + 1 selected numeric property with at least 1 selected keyframe
consumes.description: Reads app.project.activeItem (CompItem), comp.selectedLayers, layer.selectedProperties filtered to numeric propertyValueTypes, and iterates property.keySelected(k) to find chosen keyframes. Reads property.keyValue(k) for each selected keyframe to compute the new value.

controls.name: X
controls.role: enables modification of the X (index 0) dimension of keyframe values

controls.name: Y
controls.role: enables modification of the Y (index 1) dimension of keyframe values

controls.name: Z
controls.role: enables modification of the Z (index 2) dimension of keyframe values (only applied when property has 3+ components)

controls.name: edittext1
controls.role: numeric input value for the X dimension operation

controls.name: edittext2
controls.role: numeric input value for the Y dimension operation

controls.name: edittext3
controls.role: numeric input value for the Z dimension operation

controls.name: equals_button
controls.role: sets selected keyframe dimension values to the input number (replace/set operation)

controls.name: plus_button
controls.role: adds the input number to selected keyframe dimension values

controls.name: minus_button
controls.role: subtracts the input number from selected keyframe dimension values

controls.name: multiply_button
controls.role: multiplies selected keyframe dimension values by the input number

controls.name: divide_button
controls.role: divides selected keyframe dimension values by the input number; returns original value if input is zero

functions: validate active composition and selection before operating
iterate selected layers and their selected numeric properties
apply a chosen mathematical operation (set, add, subtract, multiply, divide) per enabled dimension (X/Y/Z) to each selected keyframe value
guard against division by zero by preserving the original value
write modified values back with property.setValueAtKey(k, newValue)
wrap all mutations in an undo group for one-step reversal

scriptSim.scenario: success — add 50 to X on selected Position keyframes
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'Main Comp' (1920×1080, 24fps); 1 selected layer 'Shape Layer 1' (Shape); selected property 'Transform/Position' (TwoD) with 2 keyframes at t=0 ([100,200]) and t=2 ([500,300]), both selected; X checkbox enabled, edittext1='50', plus_button clicked

notes: Only processes properties whose propertyValueType matches one of the numeric AE constant codes (6413–6417, 6423, 6424) — non-numeric or group properties are silently skipped.
Input fields are disabled by default; each is enabled only when its corresponding axis checkbox is checked.
Z-axis modification is silently skipped when the property has fewer than 3 components (e.g. TwoD Position).
parseFloat on an empty or non-numeric input returns NaN, which then falls back to 0 via the || 0 guard, so unchecked/empty dimensions effectively add/subtract 0.
All keyframes in the property are iterated (1..numKeys); only those returning true for keySelected(k) are modified — preserving timing and interpolation on all keyframes.
The panel is docked via the standard thisObj/Panel pattern; it remains persistent across compositions.
